description_long: This obelisk is a small, hand-sized object made from a dark, fine-grained stone, likely flint or chert. It has a roughly triangular, leaf-like shape with a pointed tip and a broad, rounded base, tapering smoothly along its length. The surface is irregular and uneven, with visible flake scars and a matte, rough finish, suggesting it was shaped by knapping or chipping. The color is predominantly dark gray to black, with some lighter patches and subtle variations in tone. On one side near the base, there is a white painted inscription consisting of alphanumeric characters. The object is solid and dense, with no visible ornamentation or decorative features.
description: A small, dark stone obelisk with a pointed tip, rough flaked surface, and a white painted inscription near the base.
description_view_0: Front view shows the broad, triangular face with a visible white inscription near the base.
description_view_1: Side view reveals the thin, blade-like profile and the irregular, chipped edges.
description_view_2: Back view displays the opposite broad face, lacking any visible inscription or markings.
description_view_3: Opposite side view again highlights the thinness and uneven flaked texture of the object.
attribute_inscription: White painted alphanumeric inscription 'Q323 77-47' on the front lower portion; handwritten, legible, in Latin script.
attribute_base_design: The base is rounded and slightly irregular, with no decorative features or steps; it is simply the broadest part of the stone.
attribute_tip_design: The tip is pointed and sharp, formed by converging flaked edges, with no additional decoration or finish.
